title: Determination of LD50 and LD90 for Bacterial Pathogens in Galleria mellonella
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fhn2r","text":"This protocol describes a method for determining the median and 90% lethal doses (LD₅₀ and LD₉₀) of bacterial pathogens using the Galleria mellonella larvae (greater wax moth) infection model. The protocol is optimized \nfor uropathogenic Escherichia coli (UPEC) but is readily adaptable to other bacterial species.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":130,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":238,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2n7s0","text":"Expected results include Kaplan-Meier survival curves for each bacterial dose and precise LD₅₀/LD₉₀ values with confidence intervals, calculated via Probit or Spearman-Kärber analysis. These metrics allow for direct comparison of pathogen strains and establish standardized infection doses for later experiments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ulsm","text":"Calculation of the LD50 and LD90 can also be done outside of Prism in software like MS Excel. Use this formula if you prefer a manual calculation or lack software. It requires doses that yield 0% and 100% mortality at the extremes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":21,"length":2},{"style":"sub","offset":30,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"129f8","text":"  ","type":"align-center","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"62o6i","text":"Where x_k is the lowest dose that gives 100% mortality (or the last overall), d is the Log-interval between doses (10 for 10-fold dilutions), Sum p_i is the sum of the proportions of mortality for all doses up to, but NOT including x_k. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9gcus","text":"For LD90, replace 0.5 for 0.9 in the equation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8uobc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61o61","text":"Always report:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8r4t4","text":"The timepoint used for the LD calculation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjvfb","text":"The method used (e.g., Probit analysis via GraphPad Prism).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5uh7d","text":"The LD value with its 95% Confidence Interval (e.g., LD₅₀ = 4.9 x 10⁶ CFU/larva [95% CI: 2.1x10⁶ - 1.1x10⁷]).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5skkl","text":"The sample size (n) per group.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tex_formula","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"formula":"LD_{50}=log_{10}(x_k)+(log_{10}(d)*(0.5-\\sum p_i))","guid":"","id":0}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"95g5k","text":"Ensure all equipment and reagents are sterile to prevent contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ftuq","text":"Take special care when manipulating needles and syringes to avoid accidental punctures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"165d3","text":"Sterile PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dmee","text":"2 ml Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bo2mk","text":"Crushed ice tray","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alpmi","text":"Refrigerated ultracentrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5modl","text":"Hamilton syringes with a 30G needles","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate 5 ml of appropriate sterile media with a single colony of the target E. coli strain.
Select healthy, 7th-instar larvae (>300 mg), with no signs of melanization or discoloration.
Randomly allocate 16 larvae per treatment group.
Optional: Place larvae on ice for 5-10 minutes to immobilize them.
Clean the injection site (last left proleg) with 70% ethanol and allow to air dry.
Transfer 2ml of the overnight culture to a 2 ml microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 600 x g for 10 minutes, at 4 °C.
Carefully discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 1 ml of sterile, ice-cold PBS.
Measure the OD₆₀₀. Adjust the suspension with PBS to a final OD₆₀₀ = 1.25 ≈ 1 x 10⁹ CFU/ml.

Perform 10-fold serial dilutions in sterile PBS to create the following working stocks: - Group 1: 1 x 10⁸ CFU/mL - Group 2: 1 x 10⁷ CFU/mL - Group 3: 1 x 10⁶ CFU/mL - Group 4: 1 x 10⁵ CFU/mL - Group 5: 1 x 10⁴ CFU/mL - Control: Sterile PBS only.
Keep all bacterial suspensions on ice until injection.
Incubate overnight at 37°C with vigorous shaking (200 rpm).
Using one syringe, draw up 10 µL of the appropriate suspension and inject the 10 µl of the suspension into the hemocoel via the last left proleg.
Place injected larvae in a Petri dish (max 10 larvae/dish).
Incubate dishes at 37°C under normal day/night cycle illumination.
In a clean environment, prepare one Hamilton syringe with a 30G needle for each experimental group.
Clean the syringes and needles by drawing up and down a solution of 70% ethanol several times, then allowing them to air dry.
Check larvae at the following time points post-injection: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 24, 48, 72, 96, and 120 hours.
Record the number of deaths at each timepoint until 100% mortality is achieved.
In GraphPad Prism, create a new "Survival Table".
Input the death datapoints as 1 in the respective columns. All larvae that survived until the end should be input as 0 in the final timepoint.
Generate a Kaplan-Meier survival curve. Use the Log-rank (Mantel-Cox) test to statistically compare curves between groups.
Select a single, relevant endpoint (e.g., 24h or 48h), when mortality has plateaued for mid-range doses.
In GraphPad Prism, create a new "XY Table".
Input the Log₁₀(Dose) (e.g. 6 if the concentration was 10 6 cells/ml) in the X column, and "% Mortality" in the Y column.
Go to Analysis > XY analyses > Nonlinear regression (curve fit). Choose: Dose-response - Stimulation -> Probit model. Prism will output LD₅₀ and LD₉₀ with 95% Confidence Intervals.
creator: Juan Jose Quispe Haro
